eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between an 800–900B US trade deficit at 38% and 900B–1T at 35.5% for full-year 2026, reflecting persistent structural imbalances amid robust domestic consumer demand and a strong dollar that boost imports despite Trump administration tariffs. The Bureau of Economic Analysis reported the goods and services deficit widened to $60.3 billion in March—up 4.4% from February's revised $57.8 billion—with imports rising faster than exports, annualizing Q1 at around $700 billion but tempered by historical seasonality and 2025's $901 billion total that showed limited tariff impact so far. This keeps the race tight, as delayed supply chain shifts and potential retaliation cloud shrinkage prospects; upcoming April data release in early June, along with further tariff enforcement or dollar movements, could widen the gap toward higher bins if imports accelerate or lower ones if export growth surges.

markets.question: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi's recent May 6 press conference, where he expressed openness to a third term via referendum if voters approve and warned that eastern DRC conflict with Rwanda-backed M23 rebels could delay 2028 presidential elections, has solidified trader consensus at 91.5% against his removal by year-end. Midway through his second constitutional term ending in 2028, Tshisekedi maintains firm control of Kinshasa and key institutions despite opposition outcry from figures like Martin Fayulu accusing him of power-clinging. No verified coups, impeachments, or resignations have emerged in the past 30 days, with ongoing eastern insecurity failing to threaten national leadership. Traders price in low risk of upheaval barring major scandals, military reversals, or health events before December 31.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Rainbow Six Siege Round 5 match between ENTERPRISE Esports and Weibo Gaming in the BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 13 at 4:45PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if ENTERPRISE Esports and Weibo Gaming play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Rainbow Six Siege: ENTERPRISE Esports vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Rainbow Six Siege Round 5 match between ENTERPRISE Esports and Weibo Gaming in the BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 13 at 4:45PM ET.

This market will resolve to "ENTERPRISE Esports" if ENTERPRISE Esports win the match against Weibo Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against ENTERPRISE Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Swiss Stage, ENTERPRISE Esports and Weibo Gaming collide in a high-stakes BO3 at 2-2 records, with the winner bolstering playoff aspirations among the top 8 advancers and the loser facing elimination risk. ENTERPRISE impressed early, upsetting Ninjas in Pyjamas and Team Falcons while dropping a tight decision to G2 Esports, showcasing Oceania's resilience against elite EML and SAL squads. Weibo Gaming grinded out a key victory over All Gamers but stumbled versus Twisted Minds and Virtus.pro, mirroring their APAC struggles internationally. Weibo holds the edge from a 3-1 Swiss Stage win over ENTERPRISE at last year's Munich Major, amid stable rosters and no notable patch impacts.

markets.question: Will Alexander Albon be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.title: F1 Drivers' Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship standings with 100 points after securing three consecutive Grand Prix victories from pole position, including a dominant Miami win ahead of McLaren's Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri, extending his advantage over Mercedes teammate George Russell—who trails by 20 points after a distant fourth—to solidify trader consensus at 37.5% implied probability. Russell's consistent podiums early on, including an Australian GP triumph, keep him close at 29.5%, but his teammate's superior race pace, evident in Miami where Antonelli lapped over 40 seconds quicker adjusted for strategy, fuels intra-team rivalry concerns that could split Mercedes points. McLaren's Miami podium surge boosts Norris to 11.6%, while Red Bull's struggles drop Verstappen to 6.3%, highlighting the tight intra-squad dynamic and long 24-race season keeping the title fight competitive.

markets.question: Robert Lewandowski: Anytime Goalscorer
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Deportivo Alavés and FC Barcelona, scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Robert Lewandowski is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Alavés vs. FC Barcelona - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the La Liga game between Deportivo Alavés and FC Barcelona, scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona, already crowned La Liga champions with 91 points from 35 matches and a dominant five-game winning streak including a 2-0 victory over Real Madrid, face potential rotations in their visit to relegation-threatened Alavés (18th, 37 points), who drew a vital point against rival Elche last outing amid mixed DDLWL form. Key absences shape player props: Barcelona without top scorer Lamine Yamal (16 goals) due to injury and suspended Raphinha, spotlighting Lewandowski (13 goals, recent form) and Dani Olmo; Alavés missing leading marksmen Toni Martínez (12 goals) and Lucas Boyé (11 goals) to injury, plus suspended Facundo Garcés and injured Jon Guridi, boosting props for Barcelona midfielders like Pedri and Gavi against a depleted home defense at Mendizorroza. Historical head-to-head favors Barcelona heavily (17 wins in 20).

markets.question: Will Josh Elliott win the 2026 Connecticut Governor Democratic primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Connecticut Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Governor Ned Lamont's commanding lead in the Connecticut Democratic gubernatorial primary, reflected in trader consensus at 94.5% implied probability, stems from his strong incumbency advantage, superior fundraising, and consistent polling dominance. A University of New Hampshire survey released April 23 showed Lamont at 52% among likely Democratic primary voters, versus challenger State Rep. Josh Elliott's 18%, with 30% undecided—building on an even wider February margin. Elliott, positioning as a progressive alternative, is poised to clear the 15% delegate threshold at the May 16 state convention to secure a spot on the August 11 primary ballot, marking the first gubernatorial primary challenge to a sitting Connecticut governor since 1978. While Lamont's position appears secure, scenarios like a major scandal, Elliott's fundraising surge, or convention momentum could narrow the gap in this low-turnout contest.

markets.question: Will Renato Moicano be the UFC Lightweight Champion on December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Lightweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

events.title: Who will be UFC Lightweight champion at the end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Lightweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Ilia Topuria's overwhelming trader consensus as lightweight champion through 2026 reflects his dominant run, including a first-round knockout title capture at UFC 317 after vacating featherweight, followed by a victory over former champ Charles Oliveira in his most recent defense, keeping him undefeated at 17-0 amid pound-for-pound top rankings. Justin Gaethje's solid pricing stems from his recent unanimous decision win over Paddy Pimblett for the interim title at UFC 324, positioning him for the unification main event against Topuria on June 14 at the historic UFC White House card. Arman Tsarukyan edges as the next threat after Dana White confirmed him as official backup for that bout last week, clearing prior issues and hinting at a summer return, while Oliveira's share acknowledges his elite grappling pedigree despite the setback. Benoit Saint Denis rounds out notable contenders via recent momentum, though the division's depth tempers longer-shot probabilities.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Buffalo Bills at 58% implied probability to win the AFC East, driven by their aggressive 2026 offseason overhaul after firing Sean McDermott and hiring Joe Brady as head coach, highlighted by trades for WR D.J. Moore and DE Bradley Chubb plus recent signing of LB Mike Danna from Kansas City, fortifying Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and defensive front around a defense-heavy draft class. Reigning champions New England Patriots sit close at 36%, buoyed by their 14-3 2025 campaign, deep playoff run to Super Bowl LX under Mike Vrabel, and Drake Maye's sophomore momentum despite a middling draft grade. New York Jets languish at 7% amid $48 million dead cap from parting with Aaron Rodgers and Justin Fields, leaving QB uncertainty; Miami Dolphins trail at 6% navigating new HC Jeff Hafley and an unsettled quarterback room post-Tua Tagovailoa questions.

markets.question: Will Zendaya and Tom Holland get married by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Zendaya and Tom Holland's marriage takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Zendaya and Tom Holland, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 66% implied probability for Zendaya and Tom Holland tying the knot by year-end, driven primarily by stylist Law Roach's March 2026 claim at the Actor Awards that their secret wedding "already happened," bolstered by Zendaya's apparent gold wedding band sightings at the Oscars and Paris Fashion Week. Their confirmed engagement since late 2024, five-year relationship rooted in Spider-Man co-stars, and recent low-key updates—like Tom sharing their crocheting hobby on May 5—signal strong commitment amid privacy focus. Yet, the couple's deliberate silence, including Zendaya's April refusal to confirm rumors citing parasocial boundaries, introduces uncertainty in this high-stakes personal matter, with no verified public records; late-year holidays or project press could prompt clarity.

markets.question: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game between OGC Nice and FC Metz, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if OGC Nice and FC Metz combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

markets.question: Will MetaMask launch a token by September 30, 2026?

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Metamask, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Ubisoft announce bankruptcy by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ubisoft announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of if or when the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, as a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or other individual or team which officially represents Ubisoft.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Ubisoft announce bankruptcy by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ubisoft announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of if or when the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, as a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or other individual or team which officially represents Ubisoft.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ubisoft's projected cash reserves of 1.25-1.35 billion euros by fiscal year-end March 2026—fully available to service debt maturities—drive the 97.5% trader consensus against a bankruptcy announcement by June 30, reflecting skin-in-the-game confidence in the game publisher's liquidity. January's sweeping restructuring, including cancellation of six underperforming titles and €500 million in targeted cost savings, addressed portfolio inefficiencies, while February's Q3 net bookings of €338 million beat expectations, fueled by strong Assassin's Creed franchise performance and licensing deals. Despite depressed share prices and projected €1 billion operating loss for FY2025-26, no verified insolvency signals have emerged; full-year earnings on May 20 remain a key catalyst, with severe Q4 shortfalls or refinancing snags as rare tail risks.
